questions.question: What are the three parts of the classical TTS pipeline?
options: Text frontend (normalize dates/numbers, phonemize, predict prosody) → acoustic model (text to mel spectrogram) → vocoder (mel to waveform)
questions.explanation: By 2026 end-to-end diffusion/flow-matching blurs the acoustic+vocoder split, but the three-part mental model still holds for debugging — each stage has its own failure signatures.

questions.question: What did FastSpeech 2 change versus Tacotron 2?
options: Non-autoregressive generation: a duration predictor decides how many mel frames each phoneme gets, enabling one-pass synthesis 10x faster than Tacotron's wobbly autoregressive decoding — trading a little naturalness for monotonic-alignment reliability
It moved synthesis to the cloud
It output waveforms directly from text
questions.explanation: Tacotron 2's location-sensitive attention was slow and unstable on long text. Explicit durations fixed the alignment failure mode and shipped everywhere.

questions.question: What distinguishes F5-TTS and Kokoro at the 2026 open-source frontier?
options: F5-TTS (335M): diffusion transformer over flow matching with zero-shot cloning from 5 seconds of reference, topping open leaderboards; Kokoro (82M): CPU-runnable, best-in-class real-time English-only TTS under Apache-2.0
questions.explanation: Different design points: F5 for quality and cloning, Kokoro for latency and footprint. VITS (jointly trained encoder + flow durations + HiFi-GAN) dominated the era between, 2022-2024.

questions.question: How did vocoders evolve from WaveNet to 2024?
options: WaveNet (2016, offline-only SOTA) → WaveRNN (~realtime) → HiFi-GAN (2020, 100x realtime, near-human) → BigVGAN (2022, generalizes across speakers/languages) → neural codecs like SNAC/DAC integrated with autoregressive models
questions.explanation: The end state: discrete, bit-efficient codec tokens that let TTS become token prediction — the bridge to the codec-LM speech models later in this phase.

questions.question: How is TTS quality evaluated without slow human MOS panels?
options: Reference-free neural MOS predictors (UTMOS, DNSMOS) for leaderboards, CER via running the output through Whisper as an intelligibility proxy, and SECS (speaker-embedding cosine similarity) for cloning fidelity
questions.explanation: Human MOS (1-5 crowd ratings) remains the gold standard and CMOS (A-vs-B) tightens confidence per annotation — but automated proxies are what iterate daily.
